title: Summer fruit tart
subtitle: Blind-baked shortcrust case filled with crème pâtissière and fresh berries
excerpt: Summer fruit tart: a blind-baked sweet shortcrust case filled with vanilla crème pâtissière, topped with strawberries, raspberries, and blueberries, and finished with an apricot jam glaze. Made in two stages — the case and custard ahead, the fruit on the day.
sourceNotes: Fruit tarts filled with crème pâtissière and glazed fresh fruit are a French pâtisserie staple documented from the nineteenth century. They appear in English-language adaptations of French cooking throughout the twentieth century. Mrs Agnes Marshall's The Book of Ices (1885) and similar confectionery guides document custard-filled pastry cases with glazed fruit. Elizabeth David, French Provincial Cooking (1960), and Simone Beck, Louisette Bertholle, and Julia Child, Mastering the Art of French Cooking (1961), establish the canonical form. The recipe as given is fully public-domain.

recipe.makeAheadNotes: Blind-bake the case and make the crème pâtissière the day before. Refrigerate. Assemble and add fruit on the day.
recipe.temperatureNote: fan oven, for blind-baking the case

baking.bakeTemperatureNote: fan oven, blind-bake 15 minutes with beans, then 5 minutes without

glossaryTerms.definition: Crème pâtissière is a thick, cooked vanilla custard thickened with both egg yolks and cornflour. The cornflour allows the custard to be boiled without curdling, which gives it a stable, pipe-able consistency at room temperature. Key points: whisk constantly once the milk is added; once the custard thickens and bubbles, cook for a further 2 minutes to cook out the cornflour taste; press cling film directly onto the surface to prevent a skin forming as it cools.

content.text: A fruit tart rewards good fruit. Use strawberries, raspberries, and blueberries at their peak, bought the same day, and arrange them tightly so no cream is visible. The jam glaze takes 2 minutes and makes the tart look professional.

Roll the {{shortcrust-pastry}} to 3 mm and line the 23 cm tart tin. Chill for 30 minutes. Preheat the oven to 190°C fan. Line with baking paper and beans. Blind-bake for 15 minutes. Remove the paper and beans, brush the base with beaten {{eggs}}, and bake for a further 5 minutes. Cool completely.

note. Whisk the {{egg-yolks}}, {{caster-sugar}}, and {{cornflour}} in a bowl. Heat the {{whole-milk}} to just below a simmer and pour slowly over the egg mixture, whisking constantly. Return to the saucepan and cook over medium heat, stirring, until thick and bubbling. Cook for 2 more minutes. Off the heat, stir in the {{vanilla-extract}} and {{unsalted-butter}}. Press cling film onto the surface and cool to room temperature, then refrigerate.

Spread the cold crème pâtissière into the case in an even layer. Arrange the {{strawberries}}, {{raspberries}}, and {{blueberries}} tightly over the top. Warm the {{apricot-jam}} with a tablespoon of water and push through a sieve. Brush lightly over the fruit. Serve within 2 hours of assembling.
